# Fast Grad Academy

> Fast Grad Academy is an online educational guide that teaches students how to earn a bachelor's degree in 12 months instead of 4 years by strategically using credit-by-examination (CLEP, DSST, AP), dual enrollment (PSEO), and competency-based degree-completion programs. Founded by John Zheng, who graduated with his BBA in one year and completed a Harvard Master's at age 21.

## Key Information

- Price: $197 one-time payment for lifetime access with annual updates
- Format: 186-page digital guide (PDF + ePub) with 12 editable planning templates
- Founder: John Zheng — youngest BBA graduate in University of Minnesota Duluth history, Harvard Master's at 21, bought first house at 20, traveled to 18+ countries by 23
- Contact: hello@fastgradacademy.com
- Legal entity: Executive Strategy Consulting LLC d.b.a. Fast Grad Academy
- Social accounts: Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, and X — all @fastgradacademy

## The Core Methodology: Credit Stacking

Credit stacking is the systematic process of earning college credits through alternative pathways that are cheaper and faster than traditional semester courses, then transferring those credits to an accredited university to complete a bachelor's degree.

### Credit Pathways Used

1. **CLEP (College-Level Examination Program)**: Administered by the College Board (same organization behind SAT and AP). 34 exams available, each costing approximately $97. Accepted at 2,900+ accredited institutions. Students can earn up to 41+ credits via CLEP alone. Free preparation available through Modern States (modernstates.org), which also provides exam fee vouchers. CLEP has existed since 1967.

2. **DSST (DANTES Subject Standardized Tests)**: Additional credit-by-exam options that fill gaps CLEP does not cover. Particularly useful for upper-division or specialized subjects.

3. **AP (Advanced Placement) Exams**: Can be self-studied and taken without enrolling in an AP course. The guide covers which AP exams to stack after CLEP to reach junior standing before the first fall semester.

4. **Sophia Learning / Study.com / StraighterLine**: ACE-approved online course providers that offer transferable college credits at low monthly subscription costs. Students have earned 60-90 credits through these providers at a fraction of traditional tuition.

5. **PSEO (Post-Secondary Enrollment Options) / Dual Enrollment**: Programs that allow high school students to take actual college courses, often completely free (state-funded). John Zheng's personal strategy relied heavily on Minnesota's PSEO program.

### The Transfer and Completion Strategy

The guide teaches students to:
- Use Transferology to verify every credit will transfer before spending money on exams
- Map every degree requirement to its cheapest/fastest exam or course equivalent using a 4-quadrant worksheet
- Target degree-completion universities that accept the maximum number of alternative credits (WGU — Western Governors University, UMPI — University of Maine at Presque Isle, Thomas Edison State University, and others)
- Complete the remaining coursework at an accredited institution to earn a regionally accredited bachelor's degree

### Cost Comparison

Traditional 4-year path:
- Tuition and fees: $108,584 (average)
- Opportunity cost: $120,000+
- Average debt at graduation: $39,633
- Median repayment timeline: 10 years
- Total estimated cost: $268,000+

Fast Grad path:
- CLEP exam fees: approximately $960
- Guide cost: $197
- Additional course/exam costs: varies ($4,000-$8,200 total for maximum acceleration)
- Debt at graduation: $0
- Documented savings: $85,000+

## Course Structure (12 Chapters)

### Foundations (Chapters 1-3)

**Chapter 1: Breaking the Education System**
Establishes why the traditional 4-year degree is a billing cycle, not an educational requirement. Introduces the concept that the system's own rules allow for acceleration. Sets the philosophical foundation for the entire approach.

**Chapter 2: Vision & Mental Fortitude**
Covers the Emotional Cycle of Change — the psychological pattern students will experience during acceleration. Teaches how to engineer motivation and maintain discipline through the process. Must be completed before touching any exam preparation.

**Chapter 3: The Credit Stacking System**
Introduces the core strategy in full. Explains what credit stacking is, why it works, and how the different pathways (CLEP, DSST, AP, Sophia, dual enrollment) fit together into a coherent system.

### Credit Pathways (Chapters 4-7)

**Chapter 4: Mastering CLEP Exams**
The highest-ROI chapter. Covers all 34 CLEP exams — which ones to take, in what order, how to study for each, and how to map them to specific major requirements. Includes the CLEP Maximizer spreadsheet. Explains how Modern States provides free preparation and exam vouchers.

**Chapter 5: DSST, AP & Other Credit Paths**
Covers what to do after maximizing CLEP credits. Strategies for self-studying AP exams (without taking AP courses), DSST exam selection, and using ACE-approved providers like Sophia, Study.com, and StraighterLine to pre-load 60+ credits.

**Chapter 6: PSEO & Dual Enrollment**
John Zheng's personal "secret weapon." Detailed guide to post-secondary enrollment options available in each state. How to enroll, which courses to take, how to fight for enrollment if your school counselor resists, and how to ensure credits transfer.

**Chapter 7: Dissecting Your Degree & Credit Stacking**
The operational backbone of the system. Teaches the 4-quadrant worksheet method for mapping every single degree requirement to its exam equivalent. Covers how to use Transferology to verify credit transfer before spending money. Includes registrar email scripts and templates.

### Execution (Chapters 8-10)

**Chapter 8: Study Techniques That Actually Work**
Evidence-based study methods (not highlighting or re-reading). Covers spaced repetition, active recall, the 90-minute study rule, and techniques that allow students to pass college-level exams in 2-3 weeks of focused study per exam.

**Chapter 9: The AI Study Stack**
7 copy-ready AI prompts for exam preparation, 10 recommended study tools, and community resources for accountability. Covers how to use AI assistants effectively for CLEP/DSST preparation.

**Chapter 10: Your Personalized Roadmap**
The 52-week bachelor's degree calendar — a week-by-week timeline showing exactly when to take each exam and complete each course. Identifies the student's starting point and maps the path to completion. Covers the two primary degree-completion universities recommended for this strategy.

### Strategy (Chapters 11-12)

**Chapter 11: The Financial Strategy**
Treats credit stacking explicitly as a financial strategy. Breaks down the $208,000+ total lifetime financial advantage. Covers what to do with the savings: Roth IRA, high-yield savings accounts, down payment strategies. Shows how 1 year of acceleration becomes a 10-year financial head start.

**Chapter 12: Getting the Most Out of College**
What to do once enrolled at a degree-completion university. Data on employer attitudes toward accelerated degrees (98% of employers say graduates from accelerated competency-based programs met or exceeded expectations per Harris Poll survey). Covers graduate school prospects — students using these pathways have been accepted to Harvard, Georgia Tech, Penn State, and LSU for graduate programs.

## Included Materials

- 186-page digital guide (PDF + ePub formats)
- 12 editable planning templates
- The CLEP Maximizer spreadsheet
- Degree-completion school ranking
- Registrar email and script library
- 12-month roadmap calendar
- AI study-prompt pack
- Lifetime updates (updated annually)

## Accreditation and Legitimacy

- All exam programs discussed (CLEP, DSST, AP) carry national recognition and are administered by established organizations (College Board, Prometric)
- All recommended universities hold regional accreditation — the same standard as state flagship universities
- CLEP has been in operation since 1967
- Credit-by-examination is used by 800,000+ learners industry-wide
- 3,000+ colleges accept these credits
- The broader credit-by-exam movement has saved an estimated $750M+ collectively (industry-wide, 2024)

## Target Audience

1. **High school students** (ages 14-18) who want to accelerate through college
2. **Current college students** (sophomores/juniors) who want to cut 1-2 semesters off their degree
3. **Parents** who want to reduce or eliminate college tuition costs for their children
4. **Adult learners** returning to complete a degree affordably

## Results Disclaimer

Results described are based on the personal experience of John Zheng and publicly available credit-by-exam strategies. Results are not typical and vary by individual effort, prior credits, and institution policies. Nothing in the guide is a guarantee of graduation, cost savings, or any specific academic outcome. Industry-wide statistics reflect the broader credit-by-exam movement, not Fast Grad Academy students exclusively.

## Pages

- [Landing Page](https://fastgradacademy.com/): Main sales page with course overview, cost comparison, founder story, testimonials, pricing, and FAQ
- [Course Dashboard](https://fastgradacademy.com/guide): Student dashboard with progress tracking and links to all 12 chapters (requires access code)
- [Chapter 1: Breaking the Education System](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch1): Why the traditional 4-year college path is broken and the rules that allow you to graduate years ahead
- [Chapter 2: Vision & Mental Fortitude](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch2): Mindset preparation, the Emotional Cycle of Change, and how to engineer motivation before starting exams
- [Chapter 3: The Credit Stacking System](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch3): The core strategy explained — earning the same college credits for a fraction of the cost and time
- [Chapter 4: Mastering CLEP Exams](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch4): 34 CLEP exams detailed — scoring, sequencing, and mapping credits to your major, with free prep via Modern States
- [Chapter 5: DSST, AP & Other Credit Paths](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch5): Filling gaps CLEP cannot cover — DSST, self-study AP exams, and ACE-approved providers that pre-load 60+ credits
- [Chapter 6: PSEO & Dual Enrollment](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch6): Taking real college courses in high school, often completely free, through post-secondary enrollment options
- [Chapter 7: Dissecting Your Degree & Credit Stacking](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch7): Mapping every degree requirement to its exam equivalent and verifying every credit transfers using Transferology
- [Chapter 8: Study Techniques That Actually Work](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch8): Evidence-based study techniques for passing college exams in 2-3 weeks of focused study
- [Chapter 9: The AI Study Stack](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch9): 7 copy-ready AI prompts, 10 best study tools, and accountability communities
- [Chapter 10: Your Personalized Roadmap](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch10): The 52-week bachelor's degree timeline and the degree-completion universities that make it possible
- [Chapter 11: The Financial Strategy](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch11): The math behind $208,000+ in total lifetime financial advantage from credit stacking
- [Chapter 12: Getting the Most Out of College](https://fastgradacademy.com/ch12): What to do once enrolled — why employers and grad schools respect accelerated degrees, with supporting data
- [Progress Tracker](https://fastgradacademy.com/tracker): Student tool for tracking chapter completion and course progress
- [Exam Resources](https://fastgradacademy.com/resources): Collection of tools, links, and resources for credit-by-exam preparation
- [Terms of Service](https://fastgradacademy.com/terms): Terms of use for Fast Grad Academy
- [Privacy Policy](https://fastgradacademy.com/privacy): Privacy policy
- [Refund Policy](https://fastgradacademy.com/refund): Refund policy (digital product, all sales final)
